analysis_result: RUNX1 mutation frequency in benign NF oncoprint: 2/36 = 5.6% (2 of 36 benign/atypical NF samples with WES data have RUNX1 mutations; both are p.D33N Missense_Mutation, PASS filter). If computing against 32 samples actually present in the WES maf file, frequency = 2/32 = 6.25%.
denominator_used: N=36 (benign/atypical NF cohort: Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma, Atypical_NF} AND WES='X')
assumptions_made: Benign NF oncoprint denominator is 36 (full WES-assayed benign/atypical NF cohort); 'RUNX1-mutated' counts any RUNX1 mutation (all 2 found are PASS nonsilent missense p.D33N); Tumor_Sample_Barcode in WES maf follows format 'Tumor_<WES label>'; 4 cohort samples with no mutation rows in WES maf are still counted in the denominator
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical data: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx sheet 'Clinical Data' (108 rows, 15 columns). Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label.
- WES mutations: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM5_ESM.xlsx sheet 'Supplementary Table 2' (7457 rows, 123 columns). Relevant columns: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, HGVSp_Short, FILTER.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Start: 108 samples in clinical data
- Filter 1: Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma, Atypical_NF} -> 56 samples (35 Neurofibroma + 21 Atypical_NF)
- Filter 2: WES == 'X' (WES data available) -> 36 samples
- Final benign/atypical NF cohort: n=36

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Sample barcode mapped as 'Tumor_' + WES label (e.g., WES label 1729 -> Tumor_1729)
- RUNX1 mutations identified by Hugo_Symbol == 'RUNX1'
- All mutations in file have FILTER == 'PASS'
- Variant classifications in benign cohort: Missense, Silent, Nonsense, Frame_Shift, Splice_Site, In_Frame, etc.

D. COMPUTATION
- Of 36 cohort samples, 32 have entries in the WES maf (4 samples Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414 have no mutation rows, suggesting zero called mutations or missing).
- RUNX1 mutations found: 2 mutations in 2 distinct samples
  - Tumor_1799: RUNX1 p.D33N Missense_Mutation (PASS)
  - Tumor_1901: RUNX1 p.D33N Missense_Mutation (PASS)
- Both are nonsilent missense mutations.

E. RESULT
- RUNX1 mutation frequency = 2/36 = 5.6% (based on full cohort denominator n=36)
- Alternative: 2/32 = 6.25% (based on samples with called mutations in WES maf)
- Given the oncoprint denominator convention is the labeled cohort size (n=36), the reported frequency is 2/36 = 5.6% (~6%).

F. CAVEATS
- 4 of 36 benign cohort samples (Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) had no rows in the WES maf. They could either have zero PASS-quality mutations or be missing from the deposited table. Oncoprint denominators typically match the full cohort size (36).
- Both RUNX1 mutations are the same p.D33N missense, which could reflect a recurrent hotspot-like event or a potential artifact; both PASS filter.
- Interpretation assumes the Supp Fig 3a benign NF oncoprint denominator = 36 (the stated WES=X benign/atypical NF cohort).
